folio.label: 177v
folio.l: She nys no thyngö , of swicú pacience
As was this Melibeus wyf , Prudence
By goddes bones , whan I bete myne knaues
She bryngetú me , the grete clobbed staues
And cryetú , slee the dogges euäychon
And breke he~ bothe bak , & euäy bon
And if æ any neygúebore of myne
Wol nat in chirche , to my wyf enclyne
Or be so hardy , to hire to trespace
Whan she cometú hom , she raumpetú in my face
And crietú fals coward , wreke thy wyf
By corpus bones , I wyl haue thy knyf
And æè shalt haue my distaf , & go spynne
Fro day to nygút , rigút thus she wol bigynne
Allas she seitú , æ euäe that I was shape
To wedde a mylk soppe , or a coward ape
That wol ben ouä ladde , witú euäy wygút
Thow darst nat stonde , by thy wyues rigút
This is my lif , but if that I wol figút
And out atte dore a noon , I mot me digút
Or elles I am but lost , but if that I amana &i;&i;&i;am27 The third minim appears to be underdotted. Double check manuscript.Est/ODR
Be like a wylde lyou~ , fool hardy
I wot wel , she wol do me slee , some day
Some neigúebore , and tha~ne go my way
For I am ¹ilous , witú knyf in honde
Al be it , that I dar nat , hire w stonde
For she is bygge in armes , be my feitú
That shal he fynde , æ hire mysdootú or seitú
But late vs passe a wey , fro this matere
My lord the Monk , q¶ he , be merye of chere
For ©e shuln telle a tale , trewely
Lo Rouchestre stant heere , fastby
Ride fortú myn owne lord , breke nat oure game
But by my trouthe , I knowe nat ©oure name
Wheiæä shal I calle ©ow , my lord Daun Ioún
Or Daun Thoms , or elles daun Albou~
Of what hous be ©e , by ©oure fader kyn
I vow to god , æè hast a ful faire skyn
It is a gentil pasture , there æè goost
Thow art nat like a penau~t , or a goost
Vp on my feitú , æè art som officer
Som worthy Sexteyn , or som Celerer
For by my fadres soule , as to my dome
ìè art a maister , whan æè art at home
